requirement.original: Fix 2 bare python3 calls without venv activation in tests/score-lifecycle-contract/test-lifecycle-cas.sh at lines 151 and 194. These were flagged by the style inspector as Standard 3 violations (introduced_in_diff: true) and blocked the prior cycle's close.
requirement.clarified: Wrap the bare `python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` invocations at lines 151 and 194 with a standalone subshell venv-activation pattern: `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` ... `PYEOF` followed by `)`. No other files or content change.
requirement.what: Wrap two bare python3 heredoc calls with venv activation in a standalone subshell
requirement.why: Style inspector Standard 3 violation: bare python3 may resolve to system Python rather than project venv, causing import failures
success_criteria: grep finds exactly 2 venv-wrapped python3 invocations with $FAKE_SCORES
grep finds 0 bare python3 invocations with $FAKE_SCORES

setup.reason: non-UI -- CLI; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

development_approach.strategy: At lines 151 and 194, replace the bare heredoc opener with a standalone subshell wrapper. The opening line becomes `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'`. The heredoc body is unchanged. The PYEOF delimiter line is unchanged. Add a `)` on its own line immediately after each PYEOF line.
development_approach.validation_approach: bash -n syntax check + grep count asserts + full test run exits 0

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Fix 2 bare python3 calls without venv activation at lines 151 and 194
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Both occurrences use ( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 ... ) subshell form; zero bare python3 calls remain

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed MICRO scope is correct. Raised 4 findings: AC1 grep pattern too permissive (applied: tightened to exact string + zero-bare check), ambiguity about standalone-subshell vs if-condition pattern (applied: spec now spells out standalone subshell shape), file-scope enforcement not in ACs (out_of_scope for MICRO: dev instruction is sufficient), AC2 should name exact command (applied: already explicit).

findings.issue: AC1 grep -c >= 2 too permissive; could pass with commented or duplicate matches
findings.proposed_fix: Require exactly two fixed-string matches for venv-wrapped form AND zero bare python3 matches
findings.rationale: AC1 now requires grep count = 2 for wrapped form and grep count = 0 for bare form

findings.issue: Ambiguous whether lines 151/194 use if-condition pattern or standalone subshell
findings.proposed_fix: Spell out standalone subshell block shape with opening (, heredoc body, PYEOF, closing ) on own line
findings.rationale: Spec and ticket now explicitly state standalone subshell form with ( ... ) brackets

findings.issue: Only-one-file constraint not enforced by acceptance criteria
findings.rationale: MICRO tier: dev instruction in Requirements/Non-Goals is the appropriate vehicle; a file-scope AC adds no value when the change is a 4-line targeted edit

findings.issue: AC2 should name exact command rather than just 'bash run exits 0'
findings.rationale: AC2 now names the exact command

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: AC1 tightened to exact-match count=2 + bare-count=0; spec clarified standalone subshell shape; AC2 names exact command
